types.name: Cell
types.description: Represents a cell model, which consists of a biophysics model and a topology. You can think of the biophysics model as the 'properties' of the cell, and the topology as the 'structure' of the cell.

fields.name: biophysics
fields.description: The biophysics model of the cell, which defines the properties of the cell such as its compartments, mechanisms, and parameters.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the cell within the model.

fields.name: topology
fields.description: The topology of the cell, which defines the structure of the cell such as its morphology and connectivity.

types.name: CellInput
types.description: Input for a cell model, which consists of a biophysics model and a topology. You can think of the biophysics model as the 'properties' of the cell, and the topology as the 'structure' of the cell.

inputFields.name: biophysics
inputFields.description: The biophysics model of the cell, which defines the properties of the cell such as its compartments, mechanisms, and parameters.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The unique identifier of the cell within the model.

inputFields.name: topology
inputFields.description: The topology of the cell, which defines the structure of the cell such as its morphology and connectivity.

types.name: Compartment
types.description: Represents a compartment in a biophysics model.

fields.name: color
fields.description: An optional RGBA color (list of 4 values) used to render this compartment in the UI.

fields.name: globalParams
fields.description: The non-mechanistic (global) parameters applied to this compartment.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the compartment within the model.

fields.name: mechanisms
fields.description: The set of mechanisms active in this compartment.

fields.name: sectionParams
fields.description: The mechanism-specific parameters applied to the sections of this compartment.

types.name: CompartmentInput
types.description: Input for a compartment in a biophysics model.

inputFields.name: color
inputFields.description: An optional RGBA color (list of 4 values) used to render this compartment in the UI.

inputFields.name: globalParams
inputFields.description: The non-mechanistic (global) parameters applied to this compartment.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The unique identifier of the compartment within the model.

inputFields.name: mechanisms
inputFields.description: The set of mechanisms active in this compartment.

inputFields.name: sectionParams
inputFields.description: The mechanism-specific parameters applied to the sections of this compartment.

types.name: Connection
types.description: Represents a connection of a section to its (single) parent section, defining the morphology tree.

fields.name: childEnd
fields.description: Which end of this section attaches to the parent: 0 (default) or 1.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The ID of the parent section this section connects to.

fields.name: parentLocation
fields.description: The position along the parent section where this section attaches, between 0 and 1.

types.name: ConnectionInput
types.description: Input for a connection of a section to its (single) parent section, defining the morphology tree.

inputFields.name: childEnd
inputFields.description: Which end of this section attaches to the parent: 0 (default) or 1.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: The ID of the parent section this section connects to.

inputFields.name: parentLocation
inputFields.description: The position along the parent section where this section attaches, between 0 and 1.

types.name: Coord
types.description: Represents a 3D coordinate (in space) of a point along a section.

fields.name: diam
fields.description: The diameter of the section at this point (NEURON pt3d). Falls back to the section diameter when omitted.

fields.name: x
fields.description: The x coordinate of the point.

fields.name: y
fields.description: The y coordinate of the point.

fields.name: z
fields.description: The z coordinate of the point.

types.name: CoordInput
types.description: Input for a 3D coordinate (in space) of a point along a section.

inputFields.name: diam
inputFields.description: The diameter of the section at this point (NEURON pt3d). Falls back to the section diameter when omitted.

inputFields.name: x
inputFields.description: The x coordinate of the point.

inputFields.name: y
inputFields.description: The y coordinate of the point.

inputFields.name: z
inputFields.description: The z coordinate of the point.

types.name: CreateModEnvironmentInput
types.description: Input for creating a mod environment

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent dataset of this dataset

types.name: DateTime
types.description: Date with time (isoformat)

types.name: DeleteInput
types.description: Input for deleting an object by its id

types.name: Distribution
types.description: Represents how a section parameter is distributed along a section (NEURON range variable).

fields.name: distalValue
fields.description: The value at the most distal segment (required for 'linear').

fields.name: expression
fields.description: An expression in `x` (normalized position) and `d` (path distance) (required for 'expression').

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The kind of spatial distribution.

fields.name: proximalValue
fields.description: The value at path distance 0 (required for 'linear').

fields.name: value
fields.description: The uniform value applied to every segment (required for 'uniform').

types.name: DistributionInput
types.description: Input for how a section parameter is distributed along a section (NEURON range variable). Supply the fields matching the chosen kind: 'uniform' -> value; 'linear' -> proximal_value & distal_value; 'expression' -> expression.

inputFields.name: distalValue
inputFields.description: The value at the most distal segment (required for 'linear').

inputFields.name: expression
inputFields.description: An expression in `x` (normalized position) and `d` (path distance) (required for 'expression').

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of spatial distribution.

inputFields.name: proximalValue
inputFields.description: The value at path distance 0 (required for 'linear').

inputFields.name: value
inputFields.description: The uniform value applied to every segment (required for 'uniform').

types.name: Duration
types.description: A quantity of time (``"5 ms"``, ``"2 s"``, ``"1 hour"``).

types.name: ElectricPotential
types.description: An electric potential / voltage (``"-70 mV"``, ``"5 V"``).

types.name: ElectricalConductance
types.description: An electrical conductance (``"5 nS"``, ``"2 µS"``).

types.name: Exp2Synapse
types.description: Represents an exponential synapse model, which is a type of synaptic stimulus that has an exponential rise and decay. This will be used to specify the parameters of synapses in the model.

fields.name: cell
fields.description: The ID of the cell this synapse is located on.

fields.name: delay
fields.description: Delay before the synapse activates.

fields.name: e
fields.description: Reversal potential.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the synapse within the model.

fields.name: location
fields.description: The location on the cell where the synapse is located. This can be a section name, a segment number, or a more complex specification depending on the model.

fields.name: position
fields.description: The position along the section where the synapse is located, specified as a value between 0 and 1. This is only relevant if the location is specified as a section name.

fields.name: tau1
fields.description: Rise time constant.

fields.name: tau2
fields.description: Decay time constant.

fields.name: contentType
fields.description: The content type of the file

fields.name: creator
fields.description: Who created this file

fields.name: size
fields.description: The size of the file in bytes

fields.name: views
fields.description: The file views of this file

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](https://ecma-international.org/wp-content/uploads/ECMA-404_2nd_edition_december_2017.pdf).

types.name: Length
types.description: A spatial length (``"2.5 µm"``, ``"1 mm"``, ``"3 m"``).

types.name: NetConnection
types.description: Base class for net connection parameters.

fields.name: delay
fields.description: The delay for the connection.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the connection within the model.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description: The threshold for the connection.

fields.name: weight
fields.description: The weight (conductance) of the connection.

types.name: NetConnectionInput
types.description: Input for a synaptic connection between two cells in the model. Each connection has a pre-synaptic cell (the net stimulator) and a post-synaptic cell (the synapse).

inputFields.name: delay
inputFields.description: The delay for the connection.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The unique identifier of the connection within the model.

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of connection to create.

inputFields.name: netStimulator
inputFields.description: The ID of the net stimulator that is the pre-synaptic cell in this connection.

inputFields.name: synapse
inputFields.description: The ID of the synapse that is the post-synaptic cell in this connection.

inputFields.name: threshold
inputFields.description: The threshold for the connection.

inputFields.name: weight
inputFields.description: The weight (conductance) of the connection.

types.name: NetStimulator
types.description: Represents a net stimulator in the model. This will be used to specify the parameters of stimulators in the model.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the stimulator within the model.

fields.name: interval
fields.description: Interval between spikes.

fields.name: number
fields.description: Number of spikes to emit.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Start time of the first spike.

types.name: NetStimulatorInput
types.description: Input for a net stimulator in the model. This specifies the parameters of stimulators that drive synaptic connections.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The unique identifier of the stimulator within the model.

inputFields.name: interval
inputFields.description: Interval between spikes.

inputFields.name: number
inputFields.description: Number of spikes to emit.

inputFields.name: start
inputFields.description: Start time of the first spike.

types.name: NetSynapse
types.description: Base class for synaptic stimulus parameters.

fields.name: cell
fields.description: The ID of the cell this synapse is located on.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the synapse within the model.

fields.name: location
fields.description: The location on the cell where the synapse is located. This can be a section name, a segment number, or a more complex specification depending on the model.

fields.name: position
fields.description: The position along the section where the synapse is located, specified as a value between 0 and 1. This is only relevant if the location is specified as a section name.

types.name: NetSynapseInput
types.description: Input for an exponential synapse, a synaptic stimulus with an exponential rise and decay. This specifies the parameters of synapses in the model.

inputFields.name: cell
inputFields.description: The ID of the cell this synapse is located on.

inputFields.name: e
inputFields.description: Reversal potential.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The unique identifier of the synapse within the model.

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of synapse model to use.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: The location on the cell where the synapse is located. This can be a section name, a segment number, or a more complex specification depending on the model.

inputFields.name: position
inputFields.description: The position along the section where the synapse is located, specified as a value between 0 and 1. This is only relevant if the location is specified as a section name.

inputFields.name: tau1
inputFields.description: Rise time constant.

inputFields.name: tau2
inputFields.description: Decay time constant.

types.name: Section
types.description: Represents a section of a cell's morphology, the basic structural unit of the topology.

fields.name: category
fields.description: An optional category for the section (e.g. 'soma', 'axon', 'dend'). Biophysics compartments are matched to sections by this category.

fields.name: cm
fields.description: Specific membrane capacitance (NEURON cm).

fields.name: coords
fields.description: The 3D coordinates (NEURON pt3d) describing the section's geometry. Required if length is not provided; when supplied they take precedence over length/diam. At least two points are needed to define a cable.

fields.name: dLambda
fields.description: If set, nseg is computed from NEURON's d_lambda rule (target fraction of the AC length constant at 100 Hz per segment; 0.1 is typical) and overrides the fixed nseg.

fields.name: diam
fields.description: The diameter of the section (stylized geometry). Overridden by per-point coord diameters when coords are supplied.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the section within the cell.

fields.name: length
fields.description: Length of the section (stylized geometry). Required if coords is not provided; ignored when coords are supplied.

fields.name: nseg
fields.description: The number of segments the section is discretized into (used when d_lambda is not set). NEURON convention prefers an odd count so the section has a true midpoint node.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The connection to this section's parent section. None for the root section of the cell.

fields.name: ra
fields.description: Axial resistivity (NEURON Ra).

types.name: SectionInput
types.description: Input for a section of a cell's morphology, the basic structural unit of the topology.

inputFields.name: category
inputFields.description: An optional category for the section (e.g. 'soma', 'axon', 'dend'). Biophysics compartments are matched to sections by this category.

inputFields.name: cm
inputFields.description: Specific membrane capacitance (NEURON cm).

inputFields.name: coords
inputFields.description: The 3D coordinates (NEURON pt3d) describing the section's geometry. Required if length is not provided; when supplied they take precedence over length/diam. At least two points are needed to define a cable.

inputFields.name: dLambda
inputFields.description: If set, nseg is computed from NEURON's d_lambda rule (target fraction of the AC length constant at 100 Hz per segment; 0.1 is typical) and overrides the fixed nseg.

inputFields.name: diam
inputFields.description: The diameter of the section (stylized geometry). Overridden by per-point coord diameters when coords are supplied.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The unique identifier of the section within the cell.

inputFields.name: length
inputFields.description: Length of the section (stylized geometry). Required if coords is not provided; ignored when coords are supplied.

inputFields.name: nseg
inputFields.description: The number of segments the section is discretized into (used when d_lambda is not set). NEURON convention prefers an odd count so the section has a true midpoint node.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: The connection to this section's parent section. None for the root section of the cell.

inputFields.name: ra
inputFields.description: Axial resistivity (NEURON Ra).

types.name: SectionParamMap
types.description: Represents a section parameter mapping for a biophysics model. (this will be set on the mechanisms of the compartments of the model)

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the parameter

fields.name: distribution
fields.description: How the parameter is distributed along the section (uniform by default).

fields.name: mechanism
fields.description: The governing mechanism

fields.name: param
fields.description: The name of the parameter to set.

types.name: SectionParamMapInput
types.description: Input for a section parameter mapping of a biophysics model. (this will be set on the mechanisms of the compartments of the model)

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Description of the parameter

inputFields.name: distribution
inputFields.description: How the parameter is distributed along the section (uniform by default).

inputFields.name: mechanism
inputFields.description: The governing mechanism

inputFields.name: param
inputFields.description: The name of the parameter to set.

types.name: Signal
types.description: A signal recorded during a recording session

types.name: SpecificCapacitance
types.description: A specific membrane capacitance (NEURON ``cm``; ``"1 uF/cm^2"``).

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

fields.name: files
fields.description: Subscribe to real-time file updates

fields.name: rois
fields.description: Subscribe to real-time ROI updates

fields.name: traces
fields.description: Subscribe to real-time image updates

types.name: SynapticConnection
types.description: Represents a synaptic connection between two cells in the model. This will be used to specify the connections between cells in the model, where each connection has a pre-synaptic cell (the net stimulator) and a post-synaptic cell (the synapse).

fields.name: delay
fields.description: The delay for the connection.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the connection within the model.

fields.name: netStimulator
fields.description: The ID of the net stimulator that is the pre-synaptic cell in this connection.

fields.name: synapse
fields.description: The ID of the synapse that is the post-synaptic cell in this connection.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description: The threshold for the connection.

fields.name: weight
fields.description: The weight (conductance) of the connection.

types.name: Task
types.description: A verified provenance task under which changes were made.

fields.name: agentClientId
fields.description: The executing agent client id.

fields.name: agentSub
fields.description: The executing agent user sub.

fields.name: argsHash
fields.description: The SHA-256 of the canonicalized args.

fields.name: argsHashAlgorithm
fields.description: The args canonicalization algorithm/version.

fields.name: assigner
fields.description: The root human causer.

fields.name: assignerSub
fields.description: The raw root human causer sub.

fields.name: callerSub
fields.description: The immediate causer of this hop.

fields.name: issuer
fields.description: The provenance issuer id.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The organization the task ran in.

fields.name: parentTaskId
fields.description: The immediate parent task id, if any.

fields.name: rootTaskId
fields.description: The root task id of the whole causal tree.

fields.name: taskId
fields.description: This task id.

fields.name: tokenId
fields.description: The unique single-use token id.

types.name: Temperature
types.description: A temperature (``"310 K"``, ``"37 degC"``).

fields.name: accessor
fields.description: The accessor

types.name: Topology
types.description: Represents the topology of a cell, which defines its structure as a set of connected sections.

fields.name: sections
fields.description: The list of sections that make up the cell's morphology.

types.name: TopologyInput
types.description: Input for the topology of a cell, which defines its structure as a set of connected sections.

inputFields.name: sections
inputFields.description: The list of sections that make up the cell's morphology.

fields.name: dataset

types.name: TwoDVector

fields.name: accessor
fields.description: The accessor

types.description: Temporary S3 credentials for reading a Zarr store.

fields.description: Get temporary S3 read credentials for the Zarr object.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

enumValues.name: DIRECTIVE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a directive definition.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
